title: SB 1254 (The FRESH Act) connects incarcerated individuals with vital services such as CalFresh and other resources prior to release in order to increase the success of reentry.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 1254 (The FRESH Act) allows incarcerated individuals to pre-apply for CalFresh benefits 90 days prior to their release.",
    "The FRESH Act taps into underutilized federal funding that supports CalFresh and expands access for individuals who qualify.",
    "Expanding and streamlining access to benefits for incarcerated individuals increases the success of their reentry.",
    "Food insecurity is one of the most challenging hurdles that previously incarcerated individuals face upon reentry.",
    "In 2019, roughly 1 in 5 formerly incarcerated people suffered from food insecurity.",
    "There are currently no equivalent enrollment processes for CalFresh and other supportive services for reentry.",
    "Removing barriers to necessary services will increase the likelihood of a successful reentry for previously incarcerated individuals.",
    "The FRESH Act helps reduce food insecurity in California.",
    "The FRESH Act builds upon existing work in connecting individuals with state services in an effective manner."
  ]
}
```
